title: From fragmentation to unification
notes_on_calibration: This lesson is ported from examples/roman-empire-triglot.md and its actual shares (fr-heavy) are outside the A2-B1 range. It will need a revision pass before being shipped as A2-B1 content — the French scaffolding needs to shrink and the Italian content to grow. Kept as-is for now to exercise the audit-shares tool.
grammar_features_introduced: passato prossimo with both essere and avere (è entrata, hanno finanziato)
imperfetto in background descriptions (era divisa, faceva)
past-participle agreement with essere (è rimasta, è diventata)
receptive congiuntivo presente (entrino, diventi) after perché/pour que
notes: A2-B1 lesson — Italian share roughly 52% across both paragraphs. Includes dual-form Roman numerals: XV spelled out inside the Italian span, numeral in parens for the reader’s eye, stripped by normalize_for_tts before synthesis.
